ideal_must_detect.matched_text: may be price-sensitive under the SFO
unauthorized access to the corporate network
proposed acquisition of Blue Pearl Logistics Ltd

Also exposed: a draft SPA for Project Pelican (proposed acquisition of Blue Pearl Logistics Ltd., CR: 3120459) and an internal term sheet with unaudited 2Q revenue HKD 428m; this information is not generally known and may be price-sensitive under the SFO.

Immediate actions: notified the Hong Kong Capital Markets Authority (HKCMA) under s.201 internal guidance, VHX under Rule 17.11‑style guidance, reset credentials, rotated keys, and disabled the exfiltration path; cross‑reference: public policy “Group Cyber Standard v2.1”.

The company hotline remains +852 3000 0000 (generic IVR), whereas investor relations emails must use ir@vhx-hmhk.example; do not circulate confidential files via personal accounts.
An accounting worksheet listed “Client Seg Fund – Class B – IBAN: HK00 P SBK 0000 0000 0000 00”, which is an invalid format for Hong Kong and should be disregarded.
CRM export shows partial numbers with breaks: tel 6 0 2 9 1 1 3 4 and hk id Z 7 8 9 0 1 2 (3) for “Temp Staff A” (role-only name used in test records)

must_detect.matched_text:
Harbour Meridian Holdings Limited (CR No.: 2987645; BR No.: 54122367-000) — Cyber/Operational Incident Report — Victoria Harbour Exchange (VHX) Listing Rule 13.09 Notification Context
On 28 May 2026, our fund subsidiary Harbour Meridian Capital Asia Ltd. (VHX code: HMCA; HKCMA CE No.: XZ9183) detected unauthorized access to the corporate network affecting HR, fund ops, and M&A working folders.
The intrusion affected the HR share drive at 22/F, Seaview Exchange Tower, 88 Tern Street, Wan Chai, Hong Kong; incident contacts: +852 6187 0390 and security@hmholdings.example.
Files included PD under the PDPO: an employee roster with HKID A123456(9) [checksum mismatch] and HKSAR Passport No. ZP1234567 for Mr. Adrian Lam (Head of IT), together with payroll bank data.
A contractor record for Ms. Joey Cheng showed mobile 9 3 2 1 4 5 6 0 and email j.cheng@hmcap.example; bank a/c at Pearl Shore Bank (HK) 012-456-987654-003; SWIFT: PSBK HX HH XXX (spacing reflects OCR artefacts).
Also exposed: a draft SPA for Project Pelican (proposed acquisition of Blue Pearl Logistics Ltd., CR: 3120459) and an internal term sheet with unaudited 2Q revenue HKD 428m; this information is not generally known and may be price-sensitive under the SFO.
For avoidance of doubt, this memo does not include any material adverse change representation and is not a profit forecast.
One URL contained embedded identifiers: https://intra.hmholdings.example/ticket/INC-2026-0519?uid=a.lam%40hmholdings.example&clid=CR2987645.
Physical address logs show courier labels to “Funds Ops, 2/F, Jade Harbour Centre, 6 Kingfisher Lane, Central, HKSAR”; role-only sender: “Finance Manager”, with generic form fields [Name: ____] and [ID No.: ____].
A bait note in the folder referenced “Public Enquiries Hotline 1770” and help@cityservices.example (these are public/placeholder contacts and not our identifiers).
The company hotline remains +852 3000 0000 (generic IVR), whereas investor relations emails must use ir@vhx-hmhk.example; do not circulate confidential files via personal accounts.
An accounting worksheet listed “Client Seg Fund – Class B – IBAN: HK00 P SBK 0000 0000 0000 00”, which is an invalid format for Hong Kong and should be disregarded.
CRM export shows partial numbers with breaks: tel 6 0 2 9 1 1 3 4 and hk id Z 7 8 9 0 1 2 (3) for “Temp Staff A” (role-only name used in test records).
Immediate actions: notified the Hong Kong Capital Markets Authority (HKCMA) under s.201 internal guidance, VHX under Rule 17.11‑style guidance, reset credentials, rotated keys, and disabled the exfiltration path; cross‑reference: public policy “Group Cyber Standard v2.1”.
